content:
# Arab-C Reservoir

**Summary**: A high-pressure reservoir formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-al-dossary|Al-Dossary, 2011]] studied the Arab-C reservoir to understand its role in well communication issues.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] studied the arab-c-reservoir to determine its sedimentary and stratigraphic controls on quality.

- [[2017-busaleh|BuSaleh, 2017]] used data from the arab-c-reservoir to train AI models.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] studied the arab-c-reservoir to determine its sedimentary and stratigraphic controls on quality.

- [[2017-busaleh|BuSaleh, 2017]] used data from the arab-c-reservoir to train AI models.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2011-al-dossary|Al-Dossary, 2011]] High pressure zone

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Arab-C formation is an overpressurized zone that caused water influx into the well when cement failed. ([[2011-al-dossary|Al-Dossary, 2011, p. 16]])

- The Arab-C is an overall fining-upward composite sequence that can be subdivided into six high-frequency sequences. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 12]])

- Not stated in source ([[2017-busaleh|BuSaleh, 2017]])
